I love traveling by train.
I want to see the small towns.
I enjoy walking, but I avoid taking big tours.
I have decided to rent a car, and I hope to see the coast.
I enjoy walking the small streets.
I avoid taking big guided tours.
I want to rent a car for a day.
I would like to see the coast.
I prefer taking the train to flying.
I prefer walking to driving.
I prefer small hotels to big ones.
Let me explain. I don't want a package -- I prefer planning it myself.

Good morning! I am Miguel. Let me show you our most popular trip, here, in the brochure. It is an all-inclusive package: the hotel, the meals, a guided tour every morning. You pay a deposit today, and everything is decided.
Thank you, but, let me explain. I don't want an all-inclusive package. I enjoy choosing my own dinner, and I like planning my days myself.
Of course. So how do you like to travel between the cities? We can book flights for you.
No flights between cities. I prefer taking the train to flying, I love watching the countryside go by. But for the flight home, I want a nonstop one. I avoid long layovers.
Trains, and a nonstop flight home. Good. And the tours? We have a big guided tour every morning, very popular.
No, thank you. I avoid taking big guided tours. I enjoy walking the small streets with my husband. But I would like to rent a car for one day, for a day trip to the coast.
A rental car for one day, for the coast. Perfect. And your dates, do you have fixed dates?
My dates are flexible. I need to be able to change them. And can I pay only the deposit now, and decide the rest tomorrow?
Yes, only the deposit. You know, that is the clearest list I have had all month. Most people just say: book me anything.
For years my son booked everything, and I said yes to all of it. This time, I wanted to explain it myself.
Let me explain. I don't want an all-inclusive package.
I enjoy walking, and I avoid big guided tours.
I would like to rent a car for a day trip.
My dates are flexible. Can I pay only the deposit now?

Let me tell you a secret about my job. I am a travel agent, and I have two kinds of client. The first kind sits down and says: just book me something. A package. Everything decided. The flights, the hotel, the tours, the meals, all together for one price. And I do it, and it is fine, and they go, and half of them come back a little sad, because they spent two weeks on a bus with fifty strangers, doing things they do not enjoy. They saw the big landmarks and they never saw a single small street. The second kind is different. The second kind sits down and says: let me explain. I prefer trains to planes. I enjoy walking. I avoid big tours. I want to rent a car for one day. I need flexible dates. And that person -- that person always gets the trip they wanted, because they told me what it was. Here is the thing nobody believes: the second kind is not the one with the best English. Some of them have terrible English. But they know three little verbs -- I like, I prefer, I want -- and they are not afraid to use them. So please, when you sit down with me, do not say just book me anything. Tell me what you love doing, and what you cannot stand. Say let me explain. It is not a bother. It is my favorite sentence in the world, because it is the moment the trip becomes yours instead of mine.

My name is Teresa, I am seventy-four, and for most of my life other people planned my trips. My husband booked everything. Then my daughter booked everything. They chose the hotels, they chose the tours, they chose the restaurants, and I said yes to all of it. Yes, yes, yes. I went to beautiful places and I did things I did not enjoy, because it was easier to say yes than to explain. And do you know what changed it? Not a course. Not a grammar book. Three verbs. One afternoon, in a small office in Lisbon, an agent asked me what I wanted, and instead of saying yes, I said: let me explain. I love walking. I prefer small hotels to big ones. I avoid guided tours, and I do not want an all-inclusive package -- I like choosing my own dinner. He wrote it all down, and he built me the trip I had wanted for forty years and never asked for. I cried a little in that office, and he pretended not to see. So this is what I want to tell you, especially if you are a woman my age who has always let somebody else decide. You do not need perfect English to say what you like. You need three verbs and a little courage. I like. I prefer. I want. Learn to finish those three sentences, and no travel agent, in any country, will ever choose your life for you again.
